key_insight: A SCAPS-1D plus machine-learning workflow can rapidly rank multilayer perovskite solar-cell stacks and pinpoint high-efficiency, lead-free architectures with physically interpretable design rules.
engineering_relevance: The approach reduces the need for exhaustive device simulations and experiments, helping engineers prioritize ETL/absorber/HTL combinations for efficient and environmentally friendlier perovskite solar cells.
method: Simulated 125 ETL/absorber/HTL device architectures with SCAPS-1D.
Trained an ML model on a representative subset to predict PCE for unexplored stacks.
Used SHAP to identify the most influential descriptors and validate top candidates with full-device simulations.
evidence_signal: Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation showed reliable ranking via Spearman correlation, and the top predicted devices reached 28.85% PCE for FTO/TiO2/Cs2AgBiBr6/NiO/Ag and 28.62% for FTO/SnO2/Cs2AgInBr6/NiO/Ag.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the simulated training set, so predicted rankings may need revalidation before experimental translation.
paper_title: Multilayer Screening of Double and Conventional Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D and Machine Learning: Optimization of ETL, HTL, and Absorber for High-Efficiency Architectures
